text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

Hello and welcome back toi’s live coverage from the final day of theOlympic Games in Paris.

Great Britainhave finished this summer’s Olympics with a total of 65 medals, one more than their tally from Tokyo 2020, while level with the amount they achieved at London 2012.

Emma Finucane became the first British woman in 60 years to win three medals at a single Games as she took bronze in the individual sprint.

Elsewhere, weightlifter Emily Campbell also won bronze to add to her silver from Tokyo in the women’s +81kg category, while Kerenza Bryson finished ninth in the modern pentathlon after defending champion Katie French pulled out through illness.

We’ll have updates from across Paris today, including tonight’s closing ceremony which begins at 7pm BST.

This live blog has now closed.

Au revoir Paris. Good luck Los Angeles following this. The stars of the show where the landmarks we all know and love, the Eiffel Tower, an even better site for beach volleyball than the Copacabana in Rio. The Grand Palais was, well, palatial. Watching taekwondo will never be the same again.

The Place de la Concorde, a bloody terminus for many two centuries ago, was party central, the skateboarders and breakdancers bringing alltheir counter-culture vibesto the heart of the city. Once the rain that blighted the audacious opening ceremony, and the events utilising the Seine, had passed, the city of light was flooded with sunshine, and at its vivid centre, the Olympic cauldron would rise at night, a golden planet above the Jardin de Tuileries.

Yes the Olympic Village took some hits.The food wasn’t great, but hey, it was even worse in the media centre. And the women’s boxing was dominated by a gender dispute that yielded only losers. Those elements apart,Paris was a triumph.

Team GB did their job,surpassing the medal target of 63, and though Britain were slightly shy of the projection for gold, they were so close in at least six events it didn’t seem to matter. Besides, more than any other games, Paris opened its arms to such a degree it felt like a win just being here.

And then there was the new queen of British Athletics, a golden girl for the ages,Keely Hodgkinson, finally turning silver into gold in the 800m. A bientot.

It is finally over. You can’t really keep going afterMy Way, the old wedding-ender.Bonne nuit, tout le monde.

The Games are official over. Yet the ceremony goes on.

Yseult, a French singer-songwriter who came out of a French X-Factor style show, is singingMy Way.

Most sensible fans are making their way home.

That’s Billie Eilish, I believe. All I really know is it’s nearly midnight in Paris and she’s not Celine Dion.

Snoop Dogg, by the way, is 52 years old.

If I had paid a lot of money for tickets to a closing ceremony, I would be pretty furious that the biggest names, including surprise performer Dr Dre, are performing on a beach 5,000 miles away.

Tom Cruise is on the roof. And now he’s on the ground (safely).

I spotted GB rower Oliver Wynne-Griffith getting a selfie with him, among hundreds of others – and now he has pinched the flag off Simone Biles and put it on a motorcycle.

Mission…. possible?Tom Cruise makes a stunning entrance from the roof of the Stade de France! 💥#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/rJyoTJRCeo

I should have timed this US national anthem performance. Would have come in handy for betting on the one at the Los Angeles opening ceremony. One for BBC iPlayer research.

A surprise appearance by Simone Biles, which is great – although worryingly she has her left foot in a protective boot. Was troubled by her left foot during the gymnastics, but competed through the pain.

At the opening ceremony, they hung the Olympic flag upside down. Now it has emerged entirely tangled up on itself. We await with intrigue to see if they have got it the right way up this time.

Audible groans from the crowd as IOC president Thomas Bach calls the Games “Seine-sational”. By no means the most tiresome thing he has said this evening.

No Tom Cruise or Taylor Swift as yet. These Games have been so good I’m not sure they would add much.

The stadium is flush with real heroes, medal winners and even more significantly, those who fell short.

They go home with nothing but their pride and memories. They gave it their best. No need for reproach.

Who wrote Tony Estanguet’s speech? Lyrical, whimsical, aspirational. To be fair these Games have been the equal of his soaring sentiment.

A gold medal performance by the host country, as fine an Olympics as I have covered.

“France showed up,” he said. And it did. Up there with Sydney and London.

French indie rock band Phoenix have taken things up a notch in the Stade de France with a lively performance of their hitLisztomania.

The group from Versailles, who got the Olympians jumping along to their catchy tune, were later joined by Belgian singer Angele.

French electro house artist Kavinsky also performed his classic songNightcallwhile a cloaked guitarist shredded on the guitar after Cambodian rapper Vannda performed.

Lead singer of American indie band Vampire Weekend, Ezra Koenig, then joined Phoenix to begin the handover to the States for the 2028 Los Angeles Games.

French house synth wave artist has the floor. I’m getting serious Eurovision vibes, pop music for old people essentially. Need a bit more of the breaking scene to get my knees knocking. Surely time for Snoop Dogg?

Phoenix must have had a song on Fifa 07. I feel certain.

A “golden voyager” has taken to the stage dressed in an elaborate gold costume to help tell the history of the Olympics.

The “record” section of the closing ceremony opened with dramatic music performed by an orchestra as a dazzling light show shot beams throughout the stadium.

The section pays tribute to the records set by the athletes as well as the “golden record” which served as a time capsule when it was sent into space in 1977.

After the golden voyager was lowered to the stage from the roof, a cloaked individual presented them with a Greek flag, to represent the Games ancient origin.

The voyager was later joined on stage by some acrobats who were dressed as alien-like creatures who helped him discover the Olympic rings which were hoisted from the stadium floor.

French pianist and opera singer Benjamin Bernheim then delivered a rendition ofHymn To Apolloas composer Alain Roche played on a piano while being suspended in the air.

Looking forward to the half-time analysis from Rio Ferdinand and Roy Keane. Closing ceremony of two halves eh Keano? Not sure what’s going on to be honest with you, Rio. Not sure the players are either.

I’ve never seenCirque de Soleil, but if you wrapped everyone up in toilet paper and fired up a few smoke machines, I think it would look like this.

This is all a bit conceptual for me, but the light show involving wristbands was quite impressive. Like being at a Coldplay concert. And I love Coldplay.

Here come the Brits 🇬🇧Dressed in their jazzy floral shirts, Team GB make their presence felt at the closing ceremony 🤩#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/ymbfR5shih

A golden vision, or voyager, descends from the heavens.

Feels like Cormac Mc Carthy is among us, everything has gone a murky grey, the voyager a traveller making his way along The Road.

But where does it lead?

Impartial news + intelligent debate

All rights reserved. © 2026 Associated Newspapers Limited.
title: Tom Cruise abseils off the roof at Olympics closing ceremony in incredible cameo
author: Evan Bartlett, Tom Ward
description: Follow i's live blog for updates from the final day of the Olympic Games in Paris
